entries.author: St. Ambrose of Milan
entries.title: AGAINST USURY.
entries.body: [Scripture] orders money to be returned without usury. It is a mark of kindly feeling to help one who has nothing. It is a sign of a hard nature to extort more than one has given. If one has need of your assistance because he has not enough of his own wherewith to repay a debt, is it not a wicked thing to demand under the guise of kindly feeling a larger sum from him who has not the means to pay off a lesser amount? {fn:0}

entries.author: St. Ambrose of Milan
entries.title: DEFINITION OF A VOW.
entries.body: A vow is a request for a benefit from God with a promise to give something in return. Hence, when you have obtained what you sought, it would be an ungrateful act to delay what you have promised. {fn:0}

entries.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
entries.title: SERVANTS OF GOD MAY EAT IN ANOTHER’S FIELD.
entries.body: Let all the servants of God grant [the monks] permission to enter their fields whenever they wish and to depart when well fed and satisfied. This is according to the law given to the people of Israel that no one should arrest a thief in his fields unless he wished to take something away with him. Rather, the owner of the field should permit him who had touched nothing but what he had eaten to depart free and unpunished. {fn:0}
